---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Prerana"
date: "2025-10-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
### ===============================================================
### Age–Youth Meta-Analysis (simple CHE-style)
### ===============================================================

# --- Packages ---
need <- c("readxl","dplyr","metafor","clubSandwich","forcats")
to_install <- setdiff(need, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, repos="https://cloud.r-project.org")
invisible(lapply(need, library, character.only=TRUE))

# --- Read file ---
data <- readxl::read_xlsx("output/processed_data_aw.xlsx")

# --- Basic preparation ---
dat <- data %>%
  rename(
    StudyID   = `1_ID`,
    SampleID  = `1.1_CaseID`,
    ESID      = RowID,
    n         = `10_Size`,
    age       = `11_Mage`,
    female    = `18_Female`,
    construct = `Negative/Positive+LS`,
    income    = income_level,
    m_i       = WBmi,
    sd_i      = WBsdi,
    m_j       = WBmj,
    sd_j      = WBsdj,
    r_ij      = WBcorrij,
    timelag   = timelagc
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    n = as.numeric(n),
    age = as.numeric(age),
    female = ifelse(female > 1, female/100, female),
    r_ij = as.numeric(r_ij),
    timelag = as.numeric(timelag),
    construct = as.factor(construct),
    income = as.factor(income)
  )

# --- Compute effect sizes ---
## A. Mean-level change (SMCR)
es_ml <- escalc(
  measure = "SMCR",
  m1i = m_j, sd1i = sd_j,
  m2i = m_i, sd2i = sd_i,
  ni  = n, ri = r_ij,
  data = dat
) %>%
  mutate(type = "mean_level")

## B. Sequential correlations (Fisher z)
es_sc <- escalc(
  measure = "ZCOR",
  ri = r_ij, ni = n,
  data = dat
) %>%
  mutate(type = "seq_corr")

# Combine both
es <- bind_rows(es_ml, es_sc) %>%
  mutate(
    age_c = scale(age, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) %>% as.numeric(),
    age2 = age_c^2,
    age3 = age_c^3
  )

# --- Helper function for CHE-style model ---
fit_CHE <- function(df, rho = 0.6) {
  V <- vcalc(
    vi = df$vi,
    cluster = df$StudyID,
    subgroup = df$SampleID,
    obs = df$ESID,
    rho = rho,
    data = df
  )
  model <- rma.mv(
    yi = yi, V = V, data = df,
    random = ~ 1 | StudyID/ESID,
    method = "REML",
    sparse = TRUE
  )
  list(model=model, V=V)
}

# --- Fit base models ---
ml_data <- es %>% filter(type == "mean_level")
sc_data <- es %>% filter(type == "seq_corr")

m_ml <- fit_CHE(ml_data)
m_sc <- fit_CHE(sc_data)

cat("\n=== Base Model: Mean-level (SMCR) ===\n")
print(summary(m_ml$model))
print(coef_test(m_ml$model, vcov="CR2", cluster=ml_data$StudyID))

cat("\n=== Base Model: Sequential (Fisher z) ===\n")
print(summary(m_sc$model))
print(coef_test(m_sc$model, vcov="CR2", cluster=sc_data$StudyID))

# --- Moderator models ---
fit_mod <- function(df, rho = 0.6) {
  V <- vcalc(
    vi = df$vi,
    cluster = df$StudyID,
    subgroup = df$SampleID,
    obs = df$ESID,
    rho = rho,
    data = df
  )
  fm <- rma.mv(
    yi ~ construct + age_c + age2 + age3 + female * income + timelag,
    V = V, data = df,
    random = ~ 1 | StudyID/ESID,
    method = "REML",
    sparse = TRUE
  )
  list(
    model = fm,
    robust = coef_test(fm, vcov="CR2", cluster=df$StudyID)
  )
}

mod_ml <- fit_mod(ml_data)
mod_sc <- fit_mod(sc_data)

cat("\n=== Moderator Model: Mean-level ===\n")
print(summary(mod_ml$model))
print(mod_ml$robust)

cat("\n=== Moderator Model: Sequential ===\n")
print(summary(mod_sc$model))
print(mod_sc$robust)



```
```{r}
### ===============================================================
### Age–Youth Meta-Analysis (CHE-style) with Safe Moderators
### File: long_format_with_newcol.xlsx
### ===============================================================


# --- Effect sizes ---
## A) Mean-level: SMCR
es_ml <- escalc(
  measure = "SMCR",
  m1i = m_j, sd1i = sd_j,  # post
  m2i = m_i, sd2i = sd_i,  # pre
  ni  = n, ri = r_ij,
  data = dat
) %>%
  mutate(type = "mean_level")

## B) Sequential: Fisher z of r_ij
es_sc <- escalc(
  measure = "ZCOR",
  ri = r_ij, ni = n,
  data = dat
) %>%
  mutate(type = "seq_corr")

# Combine and build age polynomials
es <- bind_rows(es_ml, es_sc) %>%
  mutate(
    age_c = as.numeric(scale(age, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)),
    age2  = age_c^2,
    age3  = age_c^3
  )

# Split
ml_data <- es %>% filter(type == "mean_level")
sc_data <- es %>% filter(type == "seq_corr")

# Optional: set reference levels for interpretability
ml_data <- ml_data %>%
  mutate(
    construct = fct_relevel(construct, "Positive"),
    income    = fct_drop(income)
  )
sc_data <- sc_data %>%
  mutate(
    construct = fct_relevel(construct, "Positive"),
    income    = fct_drop(income)
  )

# --- CHE base fit helper ---
fit_CHE <- function(df, rho = 0.6) {
  V <- vcalc(
    vi = df$vi,
    cluster = df$StudyID,
    subgroup = df$SampleID,
    obs = df$ESID,
    rho = rho,
    data = df
  )
  model <- rma.mv(
    yi = yi, V = V, data = df,
    random = ~ 1 | StudyID/ESID,
    method = "REML",
    sparse = TRUE
  )
  list(model = model, V = V)
}

# --- Base models (for reference) ---
m_ml <- fit_CHE(ml_data)
m_sc <- fit_CHE(sc_data)

cat("\n=== Base CHE — Mean-level (SMCR) ===\n")
print(summary(m_ml$model))
print(coef_test(m_ml$model, vcov = "CR2", cluster = ml_data$StudyID))

cat("\n=== Base CHE — Sequential (Fisher z) ===\n")
print(summary(m_sc$model))
print(coef_test(m_sc$model, vcov = "CR2", cluster = sc_data$StudyID))

# --- Safe Wald helper (fixed) ---
safe_Wald <- function(model, terms, cluster, label) {
  bnames <- names(coef(model))
  keep   <- intersect(terms, bnames)
  if (length(keep) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("[SKIP] Wald test for %s: no matching coefficients in model.\n", label))
    return(NA)
  }
  C_mat <- clubSandwich::constrain_zero(model, coefs = keep)
  clubSandwich::Wald_test(model, constraints = C_mat, vcov = "CR2", cluster = cluster)
}

# --- Moderator fit helper (error-safe) ---
fit_mod <- function(df, rho = 0.6) {
  V <- metafor::vcalc(
    vi = df$vi,
    cluster = df$StudyID,
    subgroup = df$SampleID,
    obs = df$ESID,
    rho = rho,
    data = df
  )

  fm <- metafor::rma.mv(
    yi ~ construct + age_c + age2 + age3 + female * income + timelag,
    V = V, data = df,
    random = ~ 1 | StudyID/ESID,
    method = "REML",
    sparse = TRUE
  )

  rb <- clubSandwich::coef_test(fm, vcov = "CR2", cluster = df$StudyID)
  bnames <- names(coef(fm))

  # define actual terms present
  terms_construct <- grep("^construct", bnames, value = TRUE)
  terms_age       <- intersect(c("age_c","age2","age3"), bnames)
  terms_fi        <- grep("^female:income", bnames, value = TRUE)
  terms_lag       <- intersect("timelag", bnames)

  # safe Walds
  wt_construct <- safe_Wald(fm, terms_construct, df$StudyID, "construct")
  wt_age       <- safe_Wald(fm, terms_age,       df$StudyID, "age (linear+quadratic+cubic)")
  wt_fi        <- safe_Wald(fm, terms_fi,        df$StudyID, "female × income")
  wt_lag       <- safe_Wald(fm, terms_lag,       df$StudyID, "timelag")

  list(model = fm, robust = rb,
       wald = list(construct = wt_construct, age = wt_age,
                   female_x_income = wt_fi, timelag = wt_lag))
}

  # Fit full moderator model
  fm <- rma.mv(
    yi ~ construct + age_c + age2 + age3 + female * income + timelag,
    V = V, data = df,
    random = ~ 1 | StudyID/ESID,
    method = "REML",
    sparse = TRUE
  )

  # Robust table
  rb <- clubSandwich::coef_test(fm, vcov = "CR2", cluster = df$StudyID)

  # Build term sets dynamically based on actual coefficient names
  bnames <- names(coef(fm))

  # 1) construct: all coefs starting with "construct" (excluding intercept)
  terms_construct <- grep("^construct", bnames, value = TRUE)

  # 2) age block: age_c, age2, age3 (whichever survived)
  terms_age <- intersect(c("age_c","age2","age3"), bnames)

  # 3) female × income interaction: coefs like "female:incomeX"
  terms_fi <- grep("^female:income", bnames, value = TRUE)

  # 4) timelag (single term if present)
  terms_lag <- intersect("timelag", bnames)

  

# --- Fit moderators (drop-safe) ---
mod_ml <- fit_mod(ml_data)
mod_sc <- fit_mod(sc_data)

cat("\n=== Moderators — Mean-level (SMCR) ===\n")
print(summary(mod_ml$model))
cat("\nCR2 robust table — Mean-level:\n")
print(mod_ml$robust)
cat("\nOmnibus Wald tests — Mean-level:\n")
print(mod_ml$wald)

cat("\n=== Moderators — Sequential (Fisher z) ===\n")
print(summary(mod_sc$model))
cat("\nCR2 robust table — Sequential:\n")
print(mod_sc$robust)
cat("\nOmnibus Wald tests — Sequential:\n")
print(mod_sc$wald)

```
